title: Ipomedon in drei englischen Bearbeitungen
summary: CME00025 is the repository XML for a University of Michigan manually keyed and proofread electronic text derived from selected matter in Eugen Kölbing's 1889 Ipomedon in drei englischen Bearbeitungen. The encoding contains the edition title page and printed pp. 3–358: three independently derived anonymous Middle English adaptations, A and B in verse and C in incomplete prose, plus local German apparatus. It omits the dedication, preface, CLXXXI-page introduction, pp. 359–484 notes and reference matter, blanks, and binding. Kölbing edited A from Chetham's MS 8009 (Mun.A.6.31), B from British Library Harley MS 2252 with readings from one relevant leaf of an early de Worde fragment, and C from Longleat House MS 257, formerly Bath MS 25. A complete 676-canvas BSB scan of the 1889 physical volume is cached. Chetham's complete 768-image public LUNA inventory was verified but deliberately not bulk-cached because current Manchester terms prohibit systematic extraction of substantial parts. Current complete images are unavailable for Harley MS 2252, and no exact public catalog or complete digital object was found for Longleat MS 257; selected manuscript plates in Meale's 1984 article are explicitly partial.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00025
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: Internal identifiers IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID are all CME00025; the compact header has no source description, availability statement, or responsibility list.
TEMPHEAD records a revision date of 2016-05-24; TEXT/@LANG is enm even though title and apparatus material are German.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME00025
entities.description: A public manually keyed and proofread electronic text based on selected portions of Kölbing's 1889 edition. It preserves the three adaptations and local apparatus but omits the long introduction and back matter.
notes: The U-M discovery field names Hue de Rotelande as author, but direct source and modern scholarship identify Hue as author of the Anglo-Norman source and the three English adapters as anonymous.

entities.label: Eugen Kölbing, Ipomedon in drei englischen Bearbeitungen (1889)

bibliographic.citation: Eugen Kölbing, ed., Ipomedon in drei englischen Bearbeitungen (Breslau: Wilhelm Koebner, 1889), CLXXXI + 484 pp.
bibliographic.title: Ipomedon in drei englischen Bearbeitungen

physical_description.extent: CLXXXI preliminary pages and 484 Arabic-numbered pages
notes: Texts A, B, and C occupy pp. 3–253, 257–319, and 323–358 respectively; notes, corrections, indexes, and abbreviations occupy pp. 359–484.

entities.label: Chetham's Library, MS Mun.A.6.31
entities.description: The unique manuscript witness of Ipomadon A, text-bearing on current ff. 191r–335r in a 15th-century paper miscellany. Kölbing's old foliation gives ff. 188a–332a and records a lost leaf after old f. 234.

physical_description.extent: 377 currently foliated leaves plus covers, endleaves, and flyleaves represented in the 768-image sequence
notes: The institutional list describes thirteen pieces and possible London production; that witness-production context is not assigned to the lost composition of A.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2252
entities.description: The unique manuscript witness used for Ipomydon B, on ff. 54r–84r, in the first of two late-medieval literary booklets later compiled with other matter by John Colyns.

notes: The Ipomydon booklet occupies ff. 54–85; the work itself is ff. 54r–84r. The wider composite's 1460–1540 range is not a composition date for B.

entities.label: Longleat House, MS 257
entities.description: The unique manuscript witness of prose Ipomedon C, on ff. 90r–105v. Physical losses include ff. 101 and 106; f. 106 contained the ending, so the surviving text is incomplete.

date_statements.label: proposed production window
date_statements.notes: The dating concerns manuscript production and decoration; the Yorkshire localization remains circumstantial.

holding.notes: No exact current public online item catalog was found; the generic access page is retained only as an enquiry route.

physical_description.extent: 212 leaves in the reconstructed collation, with 28 leaves lost
physical_description.format: Composite manuscript of two independently produced parts
notes: The parts appear to have been bound and illuminated together early; the volume was rebound in the 19th century.

entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde Ipomydon fragment W
entities.description: A bibliographic representation of the early printed fragment Kölbing called W and collated against B. Kölbing explicitly says only one leaf among three associated Bagford leaves belongs to Ipomydon; the other two belong to another romance. Later identification as STC 5732.5 and the exact current shelfmark require current-catalog confirmation.

date_statements.label: reported printing
date_statements.notes: The date and STC identity are later bibliographic identifications; Kölbing's directly observed relevant extent is one leaf.

notes: This entity is not modeled as the source of B; it is a later printed witness used by Kölbing for variant readings.

entities.label: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek complete scan of Kölbing's 1889 edition
entities.description: A complete 676-canvas digitization from upper cover through lower cover, including binding and exterior matter, endleaves, title and preliminaries, CLXXXI printed preliminary pages plus the facing blank labelled CLXXXII by the provider, all 484 Arabic-numbered pages, blanks, and terminal matter.

entities.label: University of Manchester LUNA complete image sequence for Chetham's MS Mun.A.6.31
entities.description: A verified 768-image public sequence covering the complete exposed object from upper cover through lower cover, including inside covers, endleaves, flyleaves, all current ff. 1r–377v, blanks, and adjacent works. Individual images and leaf-level IIIF manifests are public, but no single whole-codex Presentation manifest was found.
entities.reproduction_format: LUNA JPEG images and leaf-level IIIF Presentation manifests
notes: Ipomadon A occupies image records 393–681, current ff. 191r–335r; image 682 is explicitly labelled blank f. 335v.

The complete inventory was verified through the official LUNA search API but was not bulk-downloaded because Manchester's current terms prohibit systematic extraction and reutilization of substantial parts.

entities.label: Meale's 1984 study of the Middle English Ipomedon tradition
entities.description: A peer-reviewed article treating all three Middle English adaptations and their manuscripts; its plates reproduce selected leaves but not any complete codex.

bibliographic.citation: Carol M. Meale, 'The Middle English Romance of Ipomedon: A Late Medieval Literary Legacy,' Reading Medieval Studies 10 (1984), 136–191.
bibliographic.title: The Middle English Romance of Ipomedon: A Late Medieval Literary Legacy

entities.label: Selected manuscript plates in Meale's 1984 article
entities.description: Four relevant published black-and-white plates: Longleat MS 257 ff. 98v and 90r, Chetham's MS Mun.A.6.31 f. 191r, and Harley MS 2252 f. 54r. These images provide partial visual evidence only.

physical_description.extent: four target-work plates within a 58-page article PDF

notes: Plate captions state that reproduction was by permission of the Marquess of Bath, Chetham's Library, and the British Library; no broader image reuse license is inferred.

entities.label: Nora Mayer's 2011 edition of Ipomedon B and C
entities.description: A 148-page University of York thesis containing new editions of B and C, manuscript and linguistic descriptions, textual notes, glossary, and analysis.

bibliographic.citation: Nora Mayer, An Edition of the Ipomedon B and C Texts (PhD thesis, University of York, 2011).
bibliographic.title: An Edition of the Ipomedon B and C Texts

entities.label: Sánchez-Martí's 2005 description of Longleat House MS 257
entities.description: A fresh codicological description of the entire manuscript, its two parts, losses, decoration, dating, and provenance evidence.

bibliographic.citation: Jordi Sánchez-Martí, 'Longleat House MS 257: A Description' (2005), 79–89.
bibliographic.title: Longleat House MS 257: A Description

object.description: The single leaf Kölbing identified as belonging to Ipomydon

scope.notes: Kölbing rejects the older statement that all three associated leaves are Ipomydon; the two other leaves belong to another romance.

rights.component: U-M CME00025 text and presentation as offered
rights.basis: The U-M item labels the material public domain and links the DPLA No Copyright – United States statement.
notes: This records the provider assertion and does not merge rights in the medieval text, 1889 print, U-M encoding, or repository copy into one layer.

rights.component: Underlying 1889 printed edition and medieval texts
rights.basis: The U-M item derived from this edition expressly labels its source material public domain in the United States; the 1889 publication date is directly verified.
BSB assigns the RightsStatements.org No Copyright – Non-Commercial Use Only statement to object bsb11664485.
notes: The noncommercial condition concerns the provider's digital surrogate and does not alter the separately recorded U.S. public-domain status of the underlying 1889 edition.

rights.component: Chetham manuscript photography and LUNA delivery
rights.basis: Leaf-level LUNA metadata names Chetham's Library as rights holder; Manchester's current terms permit limited personal, educational, and noncommercial extraction but prohibit systematic extraction or reutilization of substantial parts.
notes: The Public Sector Information page says many LUNA images may be downloaded without permission fees, but it does not override the systematic-extraction prohibition or supply an open license. Publication and redistribution permission remain unresolved.

rights.component: Any Longleat-supplied microfilm PDF or manuscript reproduction
rights.basis: Longleat states that microfilm PDFs for certain manuscripts are supplied for research purposes only; no item-level license for MS 257 is published.

rights.component: Meale's article text and journal presentation
rights.basis: The CentAUR End User Agreement retains copyright and permits single copies for private research, education, and noncommercial use while restricting further reproduction and commercial use.

rights.component: Selected Chetham, Harley, and Longleat manuscript photographs
rights.basis: Plate captions credit reproduction by permission of the holding institutions or owner; neither the article nor CentAUR supplies an open image license.
notes: The local cached PDF is retained for private research; publication or redistribution of the manuscript plates requires separate clearance.

rights.component: Mayer's thesis, editions, commentary, and presentation
rights.basis: The White Rose record distributes the thesis under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 2.5.

rights.component: Sánchez-Martí article text and repository PDF
rights.basis: RUA marks the item open access but the inspected record and PDF expose no explicit reuse license; copyright is therefore not treated as waived.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript readings, rhyme-secured spellings, lacunae, and conjectures
editorial_practices.representation: Edited text with manuscript readings and alternatives in footnotes and later endnotes
editorial_practices.description: Kölbing says he did not globally regularize orthography. For A and B he changed spellings where rhyme secured the form and recorded manuscript readings; he placed only secure conjectures in the text, left other proposals to notes, and did not invent lost lines or stanzas. Notes finalized roughly two years after typesetting sometimes correct or retract the printed text.
editorial_practices.scope: All three edited adaptations, with rhyme-specific intervention in A and B

editorial_practices.source_feature: Chetham manuscript readings for Ipomadon A
editorial_practices.representation: Proof corrections supplied after comparison with the manuscript
editorial_practices.description: Kölbing credits Joseph Hall with reading the A proofs while the Chetham manuscript lay beside him and with supplying corrections.

editorial_practices.representation: Italic type marks supplied or expanded letters and syllables
editorial_practices.description: Kölbing explicitly preserves italic signaling for letters and syllables represented by abbreviations rather than presenting expansions as unmarked diplomatic text.

editorial_practices.representation: Edition title page and printed pp. 3–358 encoded; all other preliminaries and back matter omitted
editorial_practices.description: Direct structural and page-by-page comparison maps the noncontiguous encoded selection to BSB canvas 5 and canvases 189–544.

evidence.citation: General Corpus source XML for CME00025
evidence.summary: Establishes repository identity, compact source metadata, text language attribute, and exact encoded content.

evidence.citation: Structural and textual inspection of CME00025 XML against Kölbing's edition
evidence.locator: title page; DIV1/DIV2 structure; line identifiers A-1–A-8890 and B-1–B-2346; page and folio milestones; terminal prose ellipsis
evidence.summary: Verifies six DIV1, eleven DIV2, 8,890 A lines in 751 groups, 2,346 B lines in nine groups, 192 C paragraphs, notes and milestone counts, part boundaries, and exact source-page selection.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, CME00025
evidence.summary: Confirms stable item identity, edition title and publication data, public access, inherited Hue de Rotelande author field, and No Copyright – United States assertion.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, About the Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse
evidence.summary: Describes the 2016–2018 expansion as manual keying from public-domain editions with in-house proofreading and editing and basic TEI encoding.

evidence.citation: Eugen Kölbing, ed., Ipomedon in drei englischen Bearbeitungen (Breslau: Wilhelm Koebner, 1889), BSB object bsb11664485
evidence.locator: title canvas 5; preface canvases 9–10; introduction especially canvases 15–19 and 184–185; texts pp. 3–358/canvases 189–544
evidence.summary: Direct inspection verifies complete bibliographic identity and physical extent, three adaptations, witnesses and folio loci, Hall's A collation, de Worde fragment scope, editorial method, source selection, and 676-canvas complete-object coverage.

evidence.citation: BSB/DDB catalog metadata and rights statement for bsb11664485
evidence.summary: Confirms BSB object identity, 1889 imprint, CLXXXI + 484-page extent, shelfmark, URN, and No Copyright – Non-Commercial Use Only statement.

evidence.citation: Chetham's Library medieval-manuscript and digital-resource pages for MS 8009 (Mun.A.6.31)
evidence.summary: Confirms current and historical shelfmarks, second-half-15th-century date, thirteen-piece miscellany, possible London production, and Manchester digital-resource route.

evidence.citation: University of Manchester LUNA complete search inventory for Chetham's MS Mun.A.6.31
evidence.locator: 768 records; images 393–681 = ff. 191r–335r; image 682 = blank f. 335v
evidence.summary: Complete API inventory and leaf inspection confirm whole exposed-object coverage, current foliation, exact A boundaries, blank terminal verso, physical metadata, 2010 digitization, and Chetham's Library rights-holder field.

evidence.citation: University of Manchester Digital Collections, Terms and Conditions
evidence.locator: conditions on extracts and systematic extraction
evidence.summary: Permits limited personal, educational, and noncommercial extracts while prohibiting systematic extraction or reutilization of substantial parts and creation of another database.

evidence.citation: University of Manchester Library, Public Sector Information and image permissions
evidence.summary: States that many LUNA images are free to download and describes the permissions route; it does not supply an open license or override database-extraction terms.

evidence.citation: British Library, Harley MS 2252
evidence.summary: Confirms composite structure and 1460–1540 whole range, second-half-15th-century Ipomydon booklet, target ff. 54r–84r, paper support and dimensions; the linked viewer currently reports digital images unavailable.

evidence.citation: Longleat House, Access the Collections
evidence.summary: Provides the current enquiry route, warns that the public 1864 catalog microfilm is inaccurate, and says research-only microfilm PDFs of certain manuscripts may be purchased; no exact MS 257 record or eligibility statement is present.

evidence.citation: Carol M. Meale, 'The Middle English Romance of Ipomedon: A Late Medieval Literary Legacy,' Reading Medieval Studies 10 (1984), 136–191
evidence.summary: Peer-reviewed study supports three-version and manuscript context; direct plate inspection confirms Longleat ff. 98v and 90r, Chetham f. 191r, and Harley f. 54r, explicitly partial relative to each codex.

evidence.citation: University of Reading CentAUR End User Agreement
evidence.summary: Retains copyright and permits a single copy for private research, education, or noncommercial use while restricting further reproduction, systematic retrieval, and commercial use.

evidence.citation: Nora Mayer, An Edition of the Ipomedon B and C Texts (PhD thesis, University of York, 2011)
evidence.locator: especially pp. 21–27 and the editions of B and C
evidence.summary: Supports independent derivation from Hue, B and C forms, date and dialect claims, Harley and Longleat loci, manuscript losses, and modern manuscript-based editions.

evidence.citation: White Rose eTheses record and license for Mayer's thesis
evidence.summary: Confirms open download and Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 2.5 licensing.

evidence.citation: Jordi Sánchez-Martí, 'Longleat House MS 257: A Description' (2005), 79–89
evidence.summary: A fresh manuscript examination supports current and old shelfmarks, support, dimensions, collation and losses, composite production, proposed 1457–1469 dating, provenance evidence, and exact Ipomedon C locus.

evidence.citation: Repositorio Institucional de la Universidad de Alicante record for Sánchez-Martí's article
evidence.summary: Confirms repository identity, open-access availability, and direct PDF; no explicit reuse license was found in the inspected record or file.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Ipomadon
evidence.summary: Supports A's anonymous independent adaptation, late-14th to mid-15th-century date, North-Midlands association, tail-rhyme form, unique Chetham witness, 8,890 lines, and relation to Hue's source.
notes: Its terminal locator f. 335v conflicts with the LUNA image sequence, where f. 335v is explicitly blank and the text ends on f. 335r.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, The Lyfe of Ipomydon
evidence.summary: Supports B's anonymous adaptation, couplet form, Harley MS 2252 locus, early printed tradition, and relation to Hue's source.
notes: Its three-leaf early-print extent is not treated as equivalent to Kölbing's direct statement that only one of the three associated leaves belongs to Ipomydon.

open_questions.question: Why does the Database of Middle English Romance cite f. 335v as A's endpoint when the complete LUNA sequence shows the text ending on f. 335r and labels f. 335v blank?
next_steps: Ask Chetham's Library or DMR to reconcile the locator; preserve f. 335r as the directly observed text-bearing end and record f. 335v as the adjacent blank.

open_questions.question: Can a complete private or publication-quality digital copy of Chetham's MS Mun.A.6.31 be obtained without violating Manchester's systematic-extraction prohibition, and what publication terms apply?
next_steps: Use the Manchester/Chetham permissions route to request an authorized whole-object copy or written permission; do not bulk-download the 768-image public inventory meanwhile.

open_questions.question: When will complete digital images of Harley MS 2252 become available again, or can the British Library supply a mediated whole-manuscript reproduction?
next_steps: Monitor the exact BL catalog and viewer, and ask the British Library about reading-room consultation and whole-object reproduction options; retain Meale's f. 54r plate as partial only.

open_questions.question: Is Longleat MS 257 eligible for the holding's purchasable microfilm-PDF service, and would that file include the complete physical object, blanks, flyleaves, and exteriors?
next_steps: Ask Longleat Collections for item-level catalog data, availability, cost, image count, complete-object coverage, and reuse terms before ordering; retain Meale's two leaves as partial only.

open_questions.question: What current catalog record, shelfmark, surviving extent, and digital access correspond exactly to Kölbing's de Worde fragment W?
next_steps: Confirm the reported STC 5732.5 identity and shelfmark in current BL or ESTC data; distinguish Kölbing's one relevant Ipomydon leaf from two associated leaves of another romance.

open_questions.question: What item-level U-M production record explains the noncontiguous selection of the edition title and pp. 3–358?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections for selection and keying notes while preserving the directly observed source mapping.

review_notes: The cataloging subject is Kölbing's edition-level compilation; the three anonymous English adaptations, their manuscripts, Hue's Anglo-Norman source, the U-M encoding, and the repository XML remain distinct layers.

Complete-object claims are provider-relative and explicit: the BSB bundle covers all 676 canvases; Chetham covers the complete 768-image exposed page sequence but has no spine or edge views; Harley and Longleat have no obtainable complete image object at review time.
No complete Chetham cache was created under restrictive terms. The locally cached Meale plates are partial witness evidence and are not substitutes for complete manuscript facsimiles.

No reusable source-evaluation or modeling problem was found beyond the skill's existing rules on catalog boundaries, provider-relative completeness, restricted viewer extraction, current-access negatives, and separation of textual, physical, editorial, and digital layers.
